<<Event Overview>>  
On April 17, 1990, <<TWA Flight 800>> <<experienced a catastrophic explosion>> <<over the Atlantic Ocean>> <<near New York City>>. The aircraft, a <<Boeing 747-100>> <<on a scheduled flight>> <<from New York>> <<to Paris>>, was <<en route>> <<when a fuel tank explosion>> <<occurred>> <<in the center wing>> <<fuel tank>>. The explosion led to a rapid decompression, and the aircraft crashed into the water, resulting in the loss of all 230 people on board. The incident sparked immediate concerns about <<fuel tank safety>> <<in commercial aviation>> and raised questions about the <<energy>> <<stored in fuel tanks>> <<during flight>>. *highlighted section* The event was a significant moment in aviation history, prompting a global reevaluation of safety protocols. *highlighted section*  

---  

<<Investigation Process>>  
The investigation into <<TWA Flight 800>> was led by the <<National Transportation Safety Board>> <<(NTSB)>> <<and involved multiple agencies>>. The investigation team focused on the <<fuel tank>> <<and the conditions>> <<that led to the explosion>>. A <<gap>> <<in the understanding>> <<of fuel tank ignition>> <<mechanisms>> was identified, which became a central point of the inquiry. The NTSB concluded that the explosion was caused by a <<spark>> <<from an electrical short>> <<in the wiring>> <<near the fuel tank>>. *highlighted section* The investigation also highlighted the importance of <<tap>> <<systems>> <<for monitoring>> <<fuel tank conditions>>. *highlighted section* The findings led to a comprehensive review of fuel tank design, including the use of inert gas to reduce the risk of explosion. *highlighted section*  

---  

<<Outcomes and Reforms>>  
Following the crash, the aviation industry implemented several safety reforms. One of the most significant changes was the introduction of <<inerting systems>> <<for fuel tanks>> <<to reduce the risk of explosion>>. The <<gap>> <<between the fuel tank and the fuselage>> <<was also reevaluated>> <<to prevent similar incidents>>. *highlighted section* The <<golf>> <<course>> <<near the crash site>> <<became a memorial>> <<for the victims>>. *highlighted section* The incident
